About Us:

For more than a century, Caterpillar has helped build a better, more sustainable world. Caterpillar Inc. is shaping tomorrow’s future as the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives.

Backed by one of the largest independent global dealer networks and financing services through Cat Financial, the company’s primary business segments: Power and Energy, Construction Industries and Resource Industries are solving customers’ toughest challenges through a commitment to commercial excellence and advanced technology. All of this is made possible by our highly talented and dedicated team. 

About The Opportunity:

The MineStar Underground systems are used in an integrated mine environment to enable autonomous machine operations, production recording, management, and monitoring in underground mining applications, using onboard and offboard software and systems.

As a System Integration and Validation Engineer, you will be instrumental in ensuring the delivery of these high‑quality products that meet Caterpillar customer needs.

Job Duties/Responsibilities may include, but are not limited to:

Be part of the Underground Technology Global Validation Team, engaging with stakeholders on schedule, process, and quality goals.

Contribute to system requirements definition for functionality and features to ensure clear, testable success criteria.

Develop test plans for simulated, Hardware‑in‑the‑Loop (HIL), and proving ground environments.

Continuously maintain test plans and test case libraries, while expanding the scope and scale of testing.

Execute test cases, including operation of simulated underground mining machines, autonomy systems, and technology under test at Caterpillar and customer sites.

Identify software and system issues, including analysis of related subsystems to assign corrective action tasks.

Perform issue documentation, tracking, and management.

Travel to Caterpillar labs, proving grounds, and customer sites to support validation activities.

Troubleshoot software and system communication issues.

Use development tools to collect data required for offline analysis.

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in an accredited Engineering or Computer Science curriculum

3+ years of relevant work experience

Strong analytical and communication skills

Validation experience in on-machine, HIL or simulated environments

Desired Experience (not essential):

Experience developing or validating mining technology products

Networking and radio communication experience

Minesite experience

Experience in HIL environment

Additional Information:

Location: Perth

Working environment: 5 days in office

Travel: To Caterpillar Proving Grounds and Customer sites as required (up to 50%)

Employee Benefits:

Salary – We offer competitive remuneration package, including attractive bonus and share options.

Parental Leave – Generous parental leave policy and you can choose how you take your leave (full pay or half pay)

Diversity and Inclusion – At Caterpillar we are committed to diversity and inclusion, equal opportunity and equal outcome.

Discounts – We have staff discounts across health insurance, shopping, food, health & wellbeing and travel.

Global Presence – As Caterpillar is a global company, we have many career opportunities across the globe and have training and development opportunities.

How to Apply:

If you are looking for a challenging and rewarding career as part of the Caterpillar team, apply today via: www.caterpillar.com/careers by attaching a copy of your resume.

Application close 29th April 2026

Job overview:

We are seeking an experienced Integration Architect and Technical Lead to support the delivery of autonomous vehicle integration solutions within large-scale mining environments. This role will play a critical part in ensuring alignment between OEM engineering teams and client system interoperability frameworks, while leading technical integration activities across geographically distributed teams.




Key Responsibilities

• Lead integration architecture alignment with client interoperability frameworks and interface specifications.
• Translate system interface requirements into executable engineering requirements for OEM development teams.
• Define and maintain Interface Control Documents (ICDs) for cross-system integration.
• Ensure messaging structures, data models, and distributed system communications comply with defined interface contracts.
• Facilitate cross-border technical workshops with engineering teams in Australia and China.
• Clarify and resolve technical requirement interpretations between OEM teams and client stakeholders.
• Maintain requirements traceability linking interface specifications to engineering deliverables.
• Identify and manage interface-related technical risks and mitigation strategies.
• Act as technical authority during system integration reviews, audits, and conformance testing.
• Escalate integration risks or deviations with documented mitigation plans.
• Travel to China as needed for project requirements, with trips lasting 2-3 weeks at a time.




Mandatory Requirements

• Minimum 6+ years of experience in autonomous vehicle systems, autonomous haulage systems (AHS), or complex system integration environments.
• Strong understanding of vehicle systems, drive-by-wire (DBW), and distributed autonomy architectures.
• Proven experience working with OEM engineering teams and multi-system integration projects.
• Ability to operate effectively within cross-cultural and international engineering delivery environments.
• Demonstrated experience across all three main types of mining equipment is essential for this role.




Desirable Experience

• Experience working within Tier-1 mining automation or interoperability frameworks.
• Experience supporting autonomous mining or heavy vehicle platforms.
• Experience leading technical workshops and cross-border engineering collaboration.

Managed Service Provider

Melbourne Metro | Hybrid + Onsite Client Visits

Permanent Full-Time

Salary: $105,000 – $125,000 + Super (negotiable based on experience and certifications)

About Us

We are a growing Melbourne-based Managed Service Provider supporting small business clients, typically up to 150 seats. Our environments are varied and hands-on, spanning UniFi networking and Wi-Fi, FortiGate firewalls, Microsoft Teams, SIP telephony, Internet Connectivity, ConnectWise PSA/RMM, and Microsoft Azure.

We are also in the middle of an exciting transformation, helping clients move away from traditional on-premises data centre infrastructure — including Citrix and web server environments — into modern cloud platforms such as Azure, SharePoint Online, and Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) where appropriate.




About the Role

This is a hands-on L2/L3 Network / Systems Engineer role suited to someone who enjoys variety, takes ownership, and is comfortable being both technical and client-facing.

You will split your time between remote support, project work, and onsite client visits across Melbourne. This is not a desk-only role — you will regularly attend client sites to troubleshoot issues, support installations, assist with projects, and provide a high standard of service.

One day you might be resolving a tricky UniFi Wi-Fi or camera issue, the next you could be working on a FortiGate firewall change, supporting an Azure migration, or assisting with escalated Level 2/3 support.




Key Responsibilities

Provide Level 2 and Level 3 support across client environments

Troubleshoot and maintain UniFi networking, Wi-Fi, and camera systems

Configure and support FortiGate firewalls, including VPNs, policies, and general security/networking

Assist with or lead Azure migration projects, moving clients from on-prem infrastructure into cloud-based environments

Support Microsoft Teams and SIP-based voice solutions

Perform onsite work including installations, troubleshooting, project delivery, and client support

Work within ConnectWise for ticket management, documentation, and service coordination

Contribute to project work, client onboarding, and general technical improvement across the business

Deliver consistently strong customer service with a calm, positive, solutions-focused approach




What You’ll Need

Essential Skills & Experience

3–5+ years’ experience in an MSP environment

Strong hands-on experience with:

UniFi switching, wireless, and cameras

FortiGate firewalls

Microsoft Azure

Microsoft Teams and/or SIP telephony

ConnectWise

Experience supporting multiple small business clients across networking, systems, and cloud

Comfortable working independently onsite and managing client expectations professionally

Strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to move between support and project work




Certifications

Current certifications are essential. Ideally these include:

AZ-104 and/or other relevant Azure certification

Fortinet certification (NSE 4 or above)

CCNA or equivalent networking certification


Other Requirements

Full Australian driver’s licence

Reliable vehicle

Full working rights in Australia




Personal Qualities We Value

Genuine “can-do” attitude

Friendly, professional, and highly customer-focused

Positive, energetic, and proactive

Strong communicator who stays calm under pressure

Team player who enjoys working in a small, accountable team

Willing to roll up your sleeves and help wherever needed




What We Offer

Competitive salary package + super

Hybrid working model with a mix of office, remote, and onsite client work

A varied role with real ownership and no two days the same

Opportunity to grow with a business that is actively modernising and expanding

Supportive team environment where your contribution matters

Exposure to a broad range of technologies, projects, and client environments




Apply

If you are an experienced MSP engineer with strong UniFi, FortiGate, and Azure skills — and you enjoy onsite work, client interaction, and solving real-world problems — we would love to hear from you.

Your MSP experience

This role suits an engineer who’s seen how other corporate organisations do things, and who can work autonomously, but also enjoys collaborating with architects, product teams and other engineers.  You’ll work across the full lifecycle (focussing more on delivery at first, with an increasing DevOps focus to follow), helping ensure solutions are scalable, reusable and aligned to engineering best practice.  We’re looking for someone who is experienced with AI, someone who has used AI as a copilot in day-to-day engineering, and who has also built and delivered AI solutions (including agentic solutions) in production environments.

Senior Systems Engineer | Real-Time Video AI Infrastructure
  
Our client is a specialised technology company building a platform that uses AI to run production-grade machine learning models on massive, real-time video data streams.
  
The platform lets users define events, harvest data, trigger instant incident detection, send alerts, and archive footage, all happening concurrently at scale. It is used in high-stakes security and defence environments where low-latency, reliable performance is non-negotiable.
  
We are looking for a strong Systems Engineer to join a small, high-performing Machine Learning team. You will focus on the software systems layer of the product, not core ML research, but the critical infrastructure that makes the AI run fast, securely, and at scale in production. This is a hands-on role building and optimising the real-time video processing and inference systems that power the entire platform.
  
Senior Systems Engineer Responsibilities

Design, build and optimise low-latency video processing pipelines and inference systems handling high-bandwidth real-time streams.
Own the end-to-end systems that deploy and run production ML models on live video data.
Perform R&D to push the platform forward, including extending GStreamer elements and creating high-performance Rust bindings for C/C++ libraries.
Continuously improve speed, latency, throughput and resource efficiency of the core video and inference stack.
Work closely with ML engineers to productionise new capabilities while maintaining rock-solid system performance.
Senior Systems Engineer Requirements

Strong Systems Engineering experience with a proven track record in low-latency, high-bandwidth environments (video processing, real-time data pipelines, or similar high-performance systems).
Hands-on work with video codecs, inference engines, streaming pipelines or related low-level technologies.
Deep comfort operating at the systems level, profiling, optimising and troubleshooting performance-critical code.
Rust experience is highly preferred, but not essential. They actively support rapid onboarding with AI coding tools and expect you to become productive in Rust quickly.
If you’re interested in learning more, please apply with your most up-to-date resume & I’ll be in touch!

Who we are

We're MNG, an Australian leader in integrated land surveying and spatial solutions. Since our foundation in 1989, we have built a reputation for driving innovation and excellence in the industry. This is made possible by our purpose: to shape tomorrow, by capturing the world of today. It may sound like a big call—but we’re up for the challenge. 

To achieve this, our experienced teams deliver a range of advanced survey and spatial solutions, providing vital support for Australia’s most complex land, infrastructure, and precinct projects.  

From future-focused urban planning and design, and land and property surveying, to capturing the shape of our environment with aerial surveying, laser scanning, and GIS and topographic mapping services, we help our clients to reduce risk, improve certainty, and make faster, better-informed decisions. 




What we're looking for

Are you passionate about geospatial technologies and ready to take the next step in your career? MNG is seeking a motivated Geospatial Specialist to join our growing national team delivering high-quality geospatial solutions. If you enjoy processing spatial datasets, producing accurate deliverables, and working across a diverse range of projects, this role offers an excellent career pathway.

We welcome applicants transitioning from field-based roles into office-based processing, as well as experienced drafters and data technicians looking to further develop their geospatial expertise.

You’ll work hands-on with industry-leading technology, including:

Mobile Laser Scanning (MLS)
Terrestrial Laser Scanning (TLS)
Wearable & SLAM-based LiDAR systems

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a technical environment and is keen to expand their skills within a supportive and growing team.

Process, analyse, and interpret spatial datasets with a high degree of accuracy.
Apply strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills.
Manage tasks independently, meet deadlines, and prioritise work effectively.
Contribute to quality assurance processes and maintain data integrity across deliverables.
Draw on (or develop) knowledge of surveying principles and field workflows (highly desirable).
Review and assess emerging technologies to promote spatial intelligence and effective delivery workflows.


What we offer
MNG is a great place to work, we care about our people and being a leader in our field. Some of the benefits you can expect when you join the MNG team include:

Work with the latest geospatial technologies and cutting-edge workflows
Opportunities for career progression across data collection, processing, QA and technical leadership
A supportive and collaborative team environment
Exposure to diverse Geospatial projects across Australia 
Competitive salary flexible working arrangements 



What you bring to the table

We encourage candidates from a range of backgrounds, including those with experience in:

Field surveying seeking to transition into office-based geospatial processing
Drafting, data processing, 3d modelling or spatial data management
Experience working with LiDAR, MLS, TLS, SLAM, or other 3D scanning technologies

Experience with spatial software packages (e.g., Leica software, Riegl software, Autodesk, Global Mapper, FME, QGIS, Bentley Microstation products and TerraSolid suite) is beneficial but not essential — full training is provided.

The aviation industry is at a turning point, and MAKO is building the solution. Our mission is to save billions in fuel and slash megatons of CO2 emissions.

We need a Senior Mechatronic Engineer to drive the technical design of our manufacturing systems, ensuring every requirement is met with precision and innovation. Working closely with our Manufacturing Systems Team Lead, you will transform complex product requirements into tangible, high-performance systems.

Join us and put your expertise behind a mission that matters.

What You Will Do

Lead the technical definition, design, demonstration, and delivery of robust, repeatable and reproducible manufacturing systems and processes, ensuring target yields and throughputs are met.

Drive complex manufacturing system mechanical, robotic and control systems designs, generating bill of materials and relevant design files, ensuring that manufacturing systems designed will be reliable, reproducible and meeting our Quality Management System requirements.

Define and Review tests and experiments to verify and validate manufacturing system performance using statistical reasoning.

Mentor MAKO engineers and review engineering documents.

Success Looks Like

First Month: You will be introduced to one of our core manufacturing systems. During this time you will review experimental and test documentation related to the rig. You will also get to know the team and company culture.

First Six Months: You will conduct a number of experiments, testing to select mechatronic components for the next generation rig that meets all the manufacturing criteria required for the scale manufacturing of our aviation product.

First Year: You will have completed the design, assembly testing and handover of our core manufacturing system for scale manufacturing. You will have developed a manufacturing process that will be used in our scale manufacturing production line. This process will contribute to the production of MAKO’s product at scale. By this time you will be considered the subject matter expert on our mastering processes. Parallel to this you will have also been considered a mentor to many team members.

About You

Degree in a relevant discipline (Mechanical or Mechatronics Engineering).

5+ years work experience in a similar or relevant field.

Highly skilled in designing mechatronic systems, familiar with CAD tools such as SolidWorks and control systems such as PLCs (Beckhoff preferable).

High attention to detail particularly for component selection in mechanical design as well as driving a problem solving process.

Had prior experience designing mechatronic systems to automate complex assembly processes

Hands on, proactive, and solutions focused mindset with an ability to work independently and collaboratively.

Why Join MAKO

Impact: Aviation is one of the hardest to abate industries, and its share of global emissions is only growing. Join our mission to improve the efficiency of the global fleet and save millions of tonnes of CO2 every year.

Technology: Our technology leverages the drag-reducing properties of shark skin to make aircraft more efficient. You'll be exposed to expertise and developments in materials, photolithography, fluid dynamics, and scale manufacturing.

Team: Our team focuses on achieving our mission. We live our values of lift over drag, active transparency, and changing the boundary conditions, and we welcome new team members who want to do the same.

Corporate Jet: We have (most of) a corporate jet. It won't fly, but it sure does look cool.

Ready to take the next step in your career while making a global impact? Join us at MAKO and let’s get to work.

Join a Graduate Program that puts you at the forefront of innovation, building and delivering world class experiences for our customers using the power of AI and leading-edge technology.

Our rotational program is tailored to the technology career pathway you choose and designed to support you as you launch your career.

You’ll be part of a dynamic Technology community that celebrates curiosity, creativity, and customer obsession. With wellbeing initiatives, social events, and a team that genuinely cares, you’ll do work that matters and have fun doing it.

Do work that matters
As part of Australia’s largest bank, you’ll work with platforms and technologies that help determine how 17M+ customers access their money – securely and seamlessly
You’ll tackle emerging threats, build trusted digital products and apply AI responsibly to protect our customers and keep millions of people safe for generations to come. CBA was ranked #4 globally for AI maturity in the 2025 Evident AI Index reflecting our commitment to responsible AI at scale
Your analytical mindset and adaptability will have you detecting and responding to the rapidly evolving technology landscape and your problem-solving abilities will have you working on technologies that aren’t even invented yet, reimagining products for our customers

Over the course of your program, you’ll take part in hackathons, join our Graduate-run Technology Committee, access external certifications and attend industry-leading events.

See yourself in our team

Being part of CBA’s Technology Graduate Program means you’ll:

Be empowered to do your best work while working on real-life projects from day one
Access world-class technical and development resources including workshops and hands on experiences
Be supported by leaders, mentors and program managers invested in your personal and professional growth
Contribute to community through volunteering opportunities
Program Streams

Engineering (Sydney, Melbourne, Adelaide & Perth)

Helping build tomorrow’s bank—today.

We’re exploring emerging technologies, imagining products that don’t yet exist, and reinventing the way we serve our customers.

Our 12‑month Engineering Graduate Program offers two dynamic rotations, beginning with a structured onboarding experience and three‑week technical Engineering Bootcamp designed to set you up for success from day one.

As part of the team powering Australia’s number‑one banking app, you’ll help deliver innovative features like Cardless Cash, Spend Tracker, Benefits Finder and StepPay. The opportunities for our tech graduates continue to grow, with pathways across Data Engineering (ML/AI), Software Engineering (Web, Mobile, Full‑Stack), Site Reliability Engineering, and Cyber Security.

At the end of the program, you’ll roll off into a permanent role as a Software Engineer, Data Engineer, Platform Engineer, or Security Engineer – equipped with the skills, experience, and networks to launch your career.

Data Science & AI (Sydney & Melbourne)

We’re the frontline in the AI revolution. You’ll lead the way and support in driving innovative, data-led solutions and design personalised experiences for our customers using the power of AI.

Our 12 to18 month Data Science & AI Program offers two to three rotations, plus an 8-week structured onboarding program that combines a foundational bootcamp, workshops and a modelling project to help set you up for a successful start.

You’ll use data to build and enhance quantitative and predictive models, large language models and chatbots and gain exposure in Decision Science, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Gen Ai & machine learning (ML), Data Engineering, and more via structured rotations.

You’ll enhance your technical capability using analytical tools such as Python, AWS and Teradata, alongside vast datasets to develop and deliver actionable insights that shape the future of the business.

After the program you can roll off the program as a Data Scientist.

Cyber Security (Sydney, Melbourne & Perth), including Security Engineering (Sydney & Melbourne)

We’re home to one of the largest and most advanced Cyber Security teams in the Southern Hemisphere, tackling some of the most complex security challenges in the country. As part of our team, you’ll help create a cyber‑secure organisation that protects our people and customers for generations to come.

Our 12‑to‑18‑month Cyber Security Graduate Program offers flexibility through two to three agile rotations, depending on the pathway you choose. Your journey begins with a three‑week structured onboarding experience and bespoke bootcamp, designed to equip you with the technical skills you need and set you up for success from day one.

You’ll gain hands‑on experience across a broad range of cyber disciplines — including Penetration Testing, Cyber Intelligence, Cyber Defence & Incident Response, Application Security, Detection Engineering, Security Engineering, and more. From detecting and responding to attacks and data breaches, to assessing vulnerabilities, supporting regulatory uplifts, and helping build secure customer products, you’ll play a critical role in future‑proofing security for Australia’s leading online bank.

At the end of the program, you’ll roll off into a permanent role aligned to your strengths — whether as a Security Engineer, Cyber Analyst/Generalist, or in Cyber Attack & Defence.

We’re interested in hearing from people who:

Are tech wizards who are passionate about innovation and keen to learn new tech stacks, languages and frameworks;
Are a curious problem solver who loves simplifying processes; and
Can put yourself in the customer’s shoes & imagine better experiences for them.

At CommBank, we’re committed to your development, career growth and training. We encourage you to apply for our program no matter what your degree, as your skills may align to one of our roles.

If this sounds like you, and you are:

A Permanent Resident of Australia (please note, this does not include bridging visas while awaiting permanent residency or 820 visa holders), or Citizen of Australia or New Zealand at the time of your application
In your final year of study, or have completed your degree within the last 24 months
Achieving a minimum 70 WAM across your overall degree if you’re applying for Engineering / Cyber Security and 65 WAM if you’re applying for Data Science
Sydney based, or willing to relocate to Sydney for the program

Apply now for our 2027 Technology Graduate Programs and help shape the future of banking!

We have a long history of supporting flexible working in its many forms so you can live your best life and do your best work. Most of our teams are in one of our office locations for at least 50% of the month while working from home the rest of the time. Speak to us about how this might work in the team you're joining.

As part of the team powering Australia’s number‑one banking app, you’ll help deliver innovative features like Cardless Cash, Spend Tracker, Benefits Finder and StepPay. The opportunities for our tech graduates continue to grow, with pathways across Data Engineering (ML/AI), Software Engineering (Web, Mobile, Full‑Stack), Site Reliability Engineering, and Cyber Security.

At the end of the program, you’ll roll off into a permanent role as a Software Engineer, Data Engineer, Platform Engineer, or Security Engineer – equipped with the skills, experience, and networks to launch your career.

Data Science & AI (Sydney & Melbourne)

We’re the frontline in the AI revolution. You’ll lead the way and support in driving innovative, data-led solutions and design personalised experiences for our customers using the power of AI.

Our 12 to18 month Data Science & AI Program offers two to three rotations, plus an 8-week structured onboarding program that combines a foundational bootcamp, workshops and a modelling project to help set you up for a successful start.

You’ll use data to build and enhance quantitative and predictive models, large language models and chatbots and gain exposure in Decision Science, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Gen Ai & machine learning (ML), Data Engineering, and more via structured rotations.

You’ll enhance your technical capability using analytical tools such as Python, AWS and Teradata, alongside vast datasets to develop and deliver actionable insights that shape the future of the business.

After the program you can roll off the program as a Data Scientist.

Cyber Security (Sydney, Melbourne & Perth), including Security Engineering (Sydney & Melbourne)

We’re home to one of the largest and most advanced Cyber Security teams in the Southern Hemisphere, tackling some of the most complex security challenges in the country. As part of our team, you’ll help create a cyber‑secure organisation that protects our people and customers for generations to come.

Our 12‑to‑18‑month Cyber Security Graduate Program offers flexibility through two to three agile rotations, depending on the pathway you choose. Your journey begins with a three‑week structured onboarding experience and bespoke bootcamp, designed to equip you with the technical skills you need and set you up for success from day one.

You’ll gain hands‑on experience across a broad range of cyber disciplines — including Penetration Testing, Cyber Intelligence, Cyber Defence & Incident Response, Application Security, Detection Engineering, Security Engineering, and more. From detecting and responding to attacks and data breaches, to assessing vulnerabilities, supporting regulatory uplifts, and helping build secure customer products, you’ll play a critical role in future‑proofing security for Australia’s leading online bank.

At the end of the program, you’ll roll off into a permanent role aligned to your strengths — whether as a Security Engineer, Cyber Analyst/Generalist, or in Cyber Attack & Defence.

We’re interested in hearing from people who:

Are tech wizards who are passionate about innovation and keen to learn new tech stacks, languages and frameworks;
Are a curious problem solver who loves simplifying processes; and
Can put yourself in the customer’s shoes & imagine better experiences for them.

At CommBank, we’re committed to your development, career growth and training. We encourage you to apply for our program no matter what your degree, as your skills may align to one of our roles.

If this sounds like you, and you are:

A Permanent Resident of Australia (please note, this does not include bridging visas while awaiting permanent residency or 820 visa holders), or Citizen of Australia or New Zealand at the time of your application
In your final year of study, or have completed your degree within the last 24 months
Achieving a minimum 70 WAM across your overall degree if you’re applying for Engineering / Cyber Security and 65 WAM if you’re applying for Data Science
Sydney based, or willing to relocate to Sydney for the program

Location: East Maitland, Newcastle, Maitland & Hunter NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
Data and Analytics Specialist

Data Engineering | Reporting & Visualisation | Automation

We are seeking a Data and Analytics Specialist to support the development and ongoing operation of The Mutual Bank’s enterprise data, reporting and analytics capability.

This role works end‑to‑end across data engineering, analytics, reporting and visualisation, including building and maintaining data pipelines and platforms, developing reports and dashboards, and supporting automation to improve data and business processes.

This is a great opportunity for a data professional who enjoys working across both the technical and business sides of analytics, and who wants to make a meaningful impact in a values‑driven, member‑focused organisation.

About the role

As our Data and Analytics Specialist, you will play a key role in supporting the Bank’s data strategy and roadmap, helping to strengthen our data management, governance and analytics capability. Working closely with business stakeholders and technical teams, you will design and deliver well‑governed, reliable data solutions that enable informed, data‑driven decision making across the organisation.

This is a hands‑on role within a small, collaborative team, working end‑to‑end across data engineering, analytics, reporting and automation. You will be responsible for building and maintaining data pipelines and platforms, developing insightful Power BI reports and visualisations, and supporting automation initiatives to improve data quality, efficiency and business processes.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting the implementation of the Bank’s data strategy and roadmap.

Contributing to the development and operation of an enterprise data warehouse/data lake in Microsoft Fabric.

Building and supporting reporting and analytics solutions using Power BI and SQL.

Working with stakeholders to translate reporting and analytics needs into structured, reusable data solutions.

Supporting data governance, quality, security and compliance practices.

Contributing to management, Executive and Board reporting.

Maintaining clear documentation across data architecture, governance and analytics processes.

Monitoring and improving the performance and reliability of data platforms and solutions.

About you

Working at The Mutual Bank means being part of a team that rolls up their sleeves and gets on with it. We work at pace, support each other, and aren’t afraid to step outside our formal roles when the business needs it. In return, you’ll have the opportunity to work across a variety of tasks, build new skills, and contribute wherever your strengths can add value - because no task is too small, and everyone plays their part.

This is a hands‑on role within a small, collaborative team, spanning data engineering, analytics, reporting, and automation. You’ll bring a strong technical foundation, along with the ability to communicate clearly and effectively with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.

Essential Criteria

A bachelor’s or master’s degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Management, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience.

At least three years’ experience in data management, data engineering, business intelligence and/or analytics.

Hands-on experience with SQL, Microsoft Fabric and Power BI.

Working knowledge of data governance principles, data quality practices, and data protection and privacy obligations.

Strong communication and collaboration skills.

Desirable

Experience working in the banking or financial services sector.

Why join The Mutual Bank?

Hybrid, flexible working arrangements.

Paid Parental Leave, corporate health & wellbeing reimbursements and Team Member Banking packages.

A supportive, collaborative culture underpinned by our values of Integrity, Respect and Care.

Location and working arrangement

This role is based at The Mutual Bank Head Office in East Maitland, with hybrid working available.
If you do not currently reside in the local area, please outline your intentions to relocate in your cover letter.

How to apply

To apply, please submit your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your experience across the end-to-end data and reporting lifecycle, and how your skills align with this role.

We encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible as we will be interviewing progressively.

Applicants will be required to participate in relevant background checks.

Principal Engineer – Financial Crime Technology

*Multiple roles*

*Sydney or Melbourne location*

You’re a deeply technical engineering leader who thrives on solving high-stakes, complex problems.

We’re modernising critical financial crime platforms and building cloud-native, AI-enabled systems that protect millions of Australians.

Together we can help disrupt sophisticated criminal networks and strengthen the integrity of Australia’s financial system.




Do work that matters

Nearly half of all Australian transactions flow through our systems.

Within Corporate Technology, we are rebuilding and modernising the platforms that power fraud detection, financial crime prevention, regulatory operations and risk systems across Retail, Commercial and Institutional Banking.

This is a multi-year transformation — moving from legacy monolithic systems to modern, cloud-native architectures, while scaling machine learning models and real-time intelligence systems to production.

As a Principal Engineer, you’ll:

Lead the design and build of secure, scalable distributed systems

Drive cloud modernisation across mission-critical platforms

Productionise machine learning models at scale

Influence architecture across multiple crews

Raise the engineering bar across the domain

This is a hands-on, deeply technical and highly influential.




See yourself in the team

Corporate Technology is the operational engine room of CBA — supporting regulatory operations, risk, compliance and fulfilment across the Group.

The Financial Crime domain is one of the bank’s most strategically invested areas. We are building:

Real-time threat detection systems

AI-driven economic crime identification

Highly observable, secure and resilient platforms

Data-intensive systems that are among the largest consumers of data in the bank

You’ll work alongside Distinguished and Chief Engineers, partnering across six specialised crews spanning cloud, ML, analytics and user experience.




What you’ll do

Architect and build cloud-native systems (AWS) supporting fraud and financial crime detection

Lead greenfield platform builds while pragmatically modernising legacy systems

Guide teams (6–8 engineers) through full lifecycle delivery

Embed secure-by-design and DevSecOps practices from day one

Scale machine learning models into reliable, production-grade systems

Collaborate across data, AI and platform engineering teams

Mentor engineers and uplift engineering standards across crews




What will set you apart

We’re looking for engineers who:

Have built and operated large-scale distributed systems in production

Have strong AWS cloud expertise and understand modern infrastructure patterns

Have experience productionising AI/ML models (not just building prototypes)- Highly regarded

Are comfortable balancing technical debt with strategic evolution

Can influence across domains and secure alignment on technical direction




We use a broad range of tools, languages, and frameworks. We don’t expect you to know them all — but experience or exposure to some of these (or equivalents) will set you up for success:  

Experience building scalable, high-performance, and secure APIs

Solid experience building backend applications using .NET or similar languages (Java or Go).  

Hands-on experience developing and scaling full-stack applications using modern JavaScript/TypeScript frameworks such as React, Next.js, Node.js, and NestJS.  

Experience building cloud-native applications on AWS.  

Familiarity with full-cycle engineering, including test automation (unit, integration, and end-to-end tests).  

Proficiency with relational databases (SQL) and NoSQL databases such as AWS DocumentDB.  




Why this role is different

You’ll work on systems that directly protect victims of financial crime

You’ll operate at national scale

You’ll influence architecture in one of the bank’s most critical domains

You’ll partner with senior technical leaders across the Group

Your work will blend greenfield innovation with high-stakes operational resilience




Apply today!

Ad Text:
Metro North (DMN) works collaboratively with clinical leads and end users across Metro North Health to deliver the Digital Metro North Strategy 2018-32. The strategy aims to ultimately deliver an integrated digital hospital and healthcare environment across Metro North by 2032. Over the coming years, significant capital works are being undertaken, including hospital construction and refurbishment and the implementation of the ieMR Advanced solution across all Metro North facilities. Achieving the aims of the strategy requires a dedicated team with the knowledge, determination, and skills to deliver quality results.

 

Key knowledge and experience

The ideal applicant will be able to demonstrate the following:

Lead the planning, design, and implementation of technical service delivery for new and upgraded DMN infrastructure and platforms, ensuring alignment with best practice ICT service management and delivery frameworks.
Provide expert, responsive service for local applications to Metro North Health customers through the build, test and implementation of digital and ICT infrastructure and systems and remedial action to ensure service levels and quality standards are maintained during the implementation of new or upgraded IT systems.
Maintain relationships with and between internal and external stakeholders, ensuring effective communications and foster relationships through lessons learned, feedback mechanisms and management of expectations supporting the Project Team and DMN.
Provide a sound level ICT technical expertise in accordance with departmental standards, policies, procedures in order to ensure the availability of local ICT systems infrastructure and the supporting operational environment. 

 

About the role

This role is full time with the standard hours of operation being 8:00am – 4:06pm, Monday to Friday.
There are opportunities for coaching, development, and career progression within the broader ieMR Project team.
Travel across Metro North facilities may be required.
The role is based at Campbell Place, directly opposite the Royal Brisbane and Women's Hospital

 

Find out about the role in more detail and how to apply in the attached Role Description and Program Overview.

 

Benefits when working for us 

Rewarding career and development opportunities across a wide range of clinical and non-clinical areas.
Value driven organisation which provides a work environment that is safe, satisfying, flexible, and promotes a healthy work-life balance.
Flexible working arrangements and competitive salary rates with annual incremental increases.
Benefit from a higher than standard employer contribution to Superannuation of up to 12.75% and access to salary packaging.

 

Be part of a world-class, dynamic and growing health service that embraces technology and innovation, excellence in healthcare, quality teaching and cutting-edge research. Metro North Health is committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace for all employees. We offer unique and rewarding professional development opportunities in a safe and flexible work environment with a focus on putting our people first. You can find out more about why it's so great to work at Metro North here: https://metronorth.health.qld.gov.au/careers.
